title: Malveopsis fasciculata
taxonomy: Malveopsis fasciculata is a species within the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the order Malvales and the clade Rosids. At the kingdom level, Malveopsis fasciculata is classified as Plantae. Further classification places it within the class Magnoliopsida, also known as flowering plants. This comprehensive classification provides a clear understanding of Malveopsis fasciculata's place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Malveopsis fasciculata, also known as fascicled mallow, is a plant species in the Malvaceae family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions. The bioactive constituents of Malveopsis fasciculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Malveopsis fasciculata is often prepared as a tea or a tincture, or used in combination with other herbs to create salves and ointments, although its use and availability may vary.
